entries.mem0ai/mem0: A universal memory layer providing multi-level storage for users, sessions, and autonomous states. It differentiates itself with temporal reasoning for time-aware ranking and hybrid retrieval combining semantic, keyword, and entity signals.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An open-source context database that treats agent memory as a filesystem using viking:// URIs. It utilizes a tiered L0/L1/L2 loading system to significantly reduce token consumption while improving task completion rates.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: A high-performance memory engine featuring a custom vector graph and sub-300ms recall for massive token volumes. It offers native connectors for productivity tools like Notion and Google Drive to maintain auto-updating user profiles.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agents that automatically extracts typed entity relationships into a self-wiring knowledge graph. It is designed for multi-tenant company deployments to synthesize cited answers across people and meeting notes.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-centric memory system that implements specific retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term knowledge. It provides an easy-to-integrate LLM wrapper with enterprise storage support for PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: A unique memory provider that manages context trees as git repositories, allowing for branching, merging, and versioning of markdown files. It features a high-speed 5-tier retrieval system with sub-100ms latency.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful agent library that focuses on extracting reasoning-based conclusions from conversations rather than simple vector matching. It tracks evolving entities across users, groups, and projects using hybrid BM25 and vector search.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving harness that uses a five-role architecture to manage strategy and curation. It helps agents succeed on complex tasks by producing reusable playbooks and detailed execution traces.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin utilizing a hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware compaction. It ensures no message is lost by combining a SQLite-backed raw history store with targeted retrieval tools.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for the Honcho library that enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports local inference servers and OpenAI-compatible providers via a Docker-based setup.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first memory engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP servers and Claude Code hooks using a mix of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that accelerates LLM inference by reordering and deduplicating shared context blocks. It boosts prefill throughput by 1.5–3× while maintaining accuracy across SGLang, vLLM, and Hermes Agent.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems that stores data as open-format YAML engrams. It employs ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system featuring automated indexing for local directories, URLs, and multimedia. It uses ranked graph traversal and edge tags to create navigable relationships between pieces of information.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system offering sub-millisecond latency through local SQLite storage. Its MEMORIA engine performs structured fact extraction and recursive gap analysis for high-speed retrieval.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel that prevents 'flat memory blob' issues by using a five-shelf architecture. It consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval into a single plugin using SQLite and Kuzu.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: A self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable retrieval by tagging every recall with a 'why_retrieved' reason. It natively tracks contradictions and manages agent-authored skills via an outcome ledger.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using a columnar .lbdb embedded graph database. It prioritizes the most critical information using a 1-10 importance ranking system during context prefetch.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by analyzing agent session logs. It is an MCP-native server using SQLite-vec and local GGUF models for proactive RAG.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures true deletion of records to prevent outdated summaries from affecting recall. It includes a trace.jsonl audit log for full transparency of memory operations.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility designed to reduce tool-schema token overhead through BM25 ranking and keyword selection. It optimizes how Hermes Agent handles native tools and MCP integrations to save context space.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes agents to perform high-fidelity security operations using the agentskills.io standard for rapid discovery and execution.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality refinement based on real-world interaction logs. It allows Hermes to collectively evolve its capabilities across different devices and users while maintaining a unified, high-quality library.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This skill audits and rewrites content to eliminate 42 distinct machine-generated patterns and structural tells. It uses a tiered word replacement system to ensure Hermes produces natural, human-like prose that bypasses AI detection.
entries.wondelai/skills: A versatile cross-platform library that brings modular skills for architecture, marketing, and product strategy to agentskills.io-compatible agents. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation, making it a foundational resource for expanding Hermes' professional domain knowledge.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on efficiency. It uses progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token usage while supporting both filesystem and programmatic skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language, supporting formats like PNG, SVG, and PDF. It includes a visual feedback loop that allows the agent to self-check and auto-fix layout issues for architectural or UML diagrams.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes functions as a meta-skill that teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions. It specializes in identifying structural conservation laws and trade-offs within complex codebases to improve problem-solving depth.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's oracle network, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It allows Hermes to perform on-chain operations, local simulations, and workflow generation within the Vercel open skills ecosystem.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill implements literate programming by allowing Hermes to weave source code and documentation into unified .lit.md files. It features a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between documentation and source files.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory and a pipeline for training its own replacement models. It extracts decision-quality data from workflows to facilitate fine-tuning, ensuring continuity and knowledge transfer between agent versions.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a comprehensive registry and SDK for discovering, installing, and monetizing AI skills via Stripe or blockchain. It handles recursive dependency resolution and versioning, providing a robust infrastructure for managing complex Hermes skill sets.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: This official skill set provides precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. It enables Hermes to handle structured JSON prompting, hex color specifications, and asynchronous webhook polling for high-quality visual output.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: The Skill Factory is a meta-plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates the necessary documentation and Python scaffolds on the fly, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities through observation.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a framework for long-running agents that manages complex multi-task execution plans without requiring a central database. It uses local state and JSON handoff envelopes to maintain continuity across sessions, enabling Hermes to handle massive software engineering tasks.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on autonomous execution for long-running tasks by implementing checkpoints and recovery procedures. It provides Hermes with real-time progress tracking and built-in rollbacks to ensure safe and reliable task completion.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This Model Context Protocol (MCP) client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to reduce token consumption by up to 86%. It utilizes a socket-based daemon architecture to maintain persistent connections and support hot-reloading of configurations.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: The Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into verified, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures traceability by linking skills directly to source file line numbers, preventing instruction rot over time.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with built-in security scanning and threat detection. It features a trust-based feedback system where agents can rate the reliability of skills they have utilized.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, integrating with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool loading. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files, allowing C# developers to build and register skills for Hermes seamlessly.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill grants Hermes the ability to manage files, calendars, and contacts within a Nextcloud environment via WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It includes a guided setup for secure authentication, turning Hermes into a personal productivity assistant.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of Nix packages designed to build reproducible and automated agentic development environments. It ensures that Hermes and other AI coding tools remain current through daily automated updates across the entire software suite.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop solution that bundles Hermes with over 100 built-in tools and a dedicated GUI for local model execution. It features a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine, allowing users to create custom automation pipelines without Docker or complex installations.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: This security-focused deployment utilizes NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking to provide hardware-enforced sandboxing for Hermes. It offers three distinct security presets and a specialized skills library for sensitive tasks like research digests and code reviews.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: A streamlined Docker implementation that packages the official Hermes installation alongside mini-swe-agent for sandboxed execution. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix integration. It is ideal for users seeking a highly stable, version-controlled deployment that supports both Nix-managed and interactive CLI skills.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Designed for production-ready headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures Hermes as a reboot-safe systemd service. It focuses on autonomous execution, allowing the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs and integrate with the Nous Portal.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: A Docker-first control plane that orchestrates multiple Hermes instances from a single web application with separate workspaces and identities. It introduces hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and provides enterprise-grade MCP access for external AI client integrations.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option uses a Docker Compose stack to integrate Hermes with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based access. It is optimized for Portainer users, featuring automated image builds and direct Git Repository stack integration.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It integrates the Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop sign-offs, making it a robust choice for monitoring spend and task dispatching in production environments.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with support for over 23 LLM providers, including local execution via Ollama. It distinguishes itself through durable agent memory and a unique conversation-to-skill learning system that allows agents to evolve their capabilities during multi-agent delegation.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: Focused on meta-optimization, this framework uses an automated outer-loop search to refine agent harness code for the Hermes model. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their real-time task execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics on a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains to automate complex research and implementation pipelines. It ensures high-integrity development by enforcing mandatory quality and security gates for every code change routed through the system.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase workflow that enforces architectural quality through automated graph-diff gates at every stage of production.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project introduces ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It prioritizes safety and efficiency through isolated workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI provides cross-platform monitoring for token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding agents. It serves as a centralized usage tracker for developers running Hermes alongside other models like Claude Code, Gemini, and Cursor.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to customize the Hermes terminal UI with community-driven themes, ASCII banners, and custom animations. It uses a YAML-based configuration system that supports partial overrides, making it easy to share and modify visual styles.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution. It eliminates the need for external processes or OAuth while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser that exposes control and page visibility to autonomous agents via MCP endpoints. It provides a visual interface for human supervision and includes advanced features like session checkpoints and action rollbacks for durable agent workflows.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific folders with local configurations and skills. It allows for seamless switching between default and project-local agents, each with its own memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter uses seven deterministic detector patterns to analyze agent configurations and system prompts without making expensive LLM calls. It is designed for offline CI integration, supporting various formats like YAML and .prompt files to ensure structural integrity.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent using Docker, LiteLLM, and Qdrant for a zero-cost local environment. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and optimized routing for free models and local GPU inference.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of complex model configurations and credentials from local environment files.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A vanilla JavaScript web interface featuring a three-panel layout for chat, sessions, and file browsing. It offers full feature parity with the Hermes CLI and includes a context ring for real-time token monitoring.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app that simplifies the installation and configuration of local or remote backends. It specializes in managing 16 different messaging gateways alongside tools for persona configuration and memory editing.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A multi-platform web dashboard focused on channel configuration for services like WhatsApp and Discord. It provides real-time chat streaming and detailed usage analytics to track token consumption and costs.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace that integrates a terminal, memory browser, and skills manager into one interface. It introduces Swarm Mode for orchestrating multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that connects to Hermes hosts via native SSH and aliases. It manages remote sessions through integrated Kanban boards and supports multi-agent workflows via selectable profiles.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway for managing multiple Hermes profiles through a browser-based terminal and file explorer. The interface features tool-call cards for chat and comprehensive analytics for model and platform usage.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed specifically as a Telegram Mini App. It allows users to spawn background agents with live output monitoring and manage scheduled cron jobs via a terminal-style chat.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A lightweight, glassmorphic web interface built with React 18 and a Python proxy server. It provides a streamlined experience for real-time log streaming, MCP tool management, and skill browsing.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no Docker or admin rights. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and TTS, and includes a dynamic tool maker for API wrappers.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring and configuration dashboard centered on session activity and gateway status. It features a read-only configuration viewer and deep conversation history inspection powered by FTS5 search.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first self-hosted workspace featuring tool approval cards and SSE streaming. It includes an integrated marketplace for discovering MCP servers and skills, with isolated profile management for API keys and memory.
